docs.title: WWW::Playwright
docs.text: A Raku driver for Playwright. Raku owns the API surface and lifecycle. Node owns the browser.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Raku spawns a long-lived Node process, the sidecar, that imports the official playwright npm package. Raku and the sidecar talk over newline-delimited JSON-RPC on stdio.

+-----------+        NDJSON / stdio        +--------------+        +---------+
|   Raku    | ---------------------------> | Node sidecar | -----> | Browser |
| (driver)  | <--------------------------- | (playwright) | <----- |         |
+-----------+        JSON-RPC 2.0          +--------------+        +---------+
  • Raku sends one JSON-RPC request per line and reads one response per line.
  • The sidecar dispatches each request to Playwright and replies on the same line-buffered stream.
  • Browser objects (pages, locators, element handles, contexts) never cross the wire. The sidecar keeps them in a registry and mints opaque string handles (page@1, loc@7) that Raku passes back in later requests.

docs.title: Why a sidecar
docs.text: Playwright ships as a Node library with no stable cross-language RPC surface. Rather than reimplement the protocol, this dist drives the real Node package and keeps Raku as the orchestration layer. Raku starts the sidecar, feeds it requests, correlates responses, and tears it down on exit.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• WWW::Playwright - entry point: start the sidecar, launch a browser, stop.
  • WWW::Playwright::Sidecar - the transport that owns the Node process.
  • WWW::Playwright::Browser, ::Context, ::Page, ::Locator - thin Raku wrappers around sidecar handles.
  • WWW::Playwright::Exception - typed exceptions built from JSON-RPC errors.

docs.title: Action reference
docs.text:
Actions run against a Locator. Get one from $page.locator($selector), then call an action on it. Playwright auto-waits for the element to be actionable before each action.

my $page = $context.new-page;
$page.goto('file:///path/to/hello.html');

docs.title: click
docs.text:
Clicks the element.

$page.locator('#go').click;

docs.title: fill
docs.text:
Sets the value of an input, replacing any existing value.

$page.locator('#name').fill('Ada');

docs.title: type
docs.text:
Types text one key at a time, appending to the current value. Use fill to replace; use type when per-keystroke events matter.

$page.locator('#name').type('Hi');

docs.title: press
docs.text:
Dispatches a single key.

$page.locator('#name').press('Enter');

docs.title: check and uncheck
docs.text:
Tick or clear a checkbox.

$page.locator('#agree').check;
$page.locator('#agree').uncheck;

docs.title: select-option
docs.text:
Selects an option in a <select> by value and returns the chosen values.

my @selected = $page.locator('#color').select-option('green');   # ('green',)

docs.title: hover
docs.text:
Moves the pointer over the element.

$page.locator('#hoverable').hover;

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Two environment variables control how the transport finds Node and how much it reports.

docs.title: PLAYWRIGHT_NODE
docs.text:
The path to the Node binary that runs the sidecar.

When unset, the transport searches PATH for node. When set, it uses that path directly. If the resolved binary does not exist or is not executable, constructing a WWW::Playwright::Sidecar throws X::WWW::Playwright::NodeNotFound with a message pointing at this variable.

PLAYWRIGHT_NODE=/opt/node/bin/node raku your-script.raku

docs.title: PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG
docs.text:
When set to any non-empty value, the transport streams the sidecar's stderr to the Raku process stderr, each line prefixed with [playwright-sidecar].

PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG=1 raku your-script.raku

The sidecar's stderr is always captured and available through $sidecar.stderr-lines, regardless of this flag. PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG only controls whether those lines are also echoed live.

docs.title: Diagnostics
docs.text: Two tools for capturing what happened during a run: screenshots and traces.

docs.title: Screenshots
docs.text:
Page.screenshot returns the image bytes as a Buf. Pass :path to also write the image to disk.

# bytes only
my $bytes = $page.screenshot;

# write a PNG and get the bytes back
my $bytes = $page.screenshot(path => '/tmp/page.png');

The bytes are a PNG, so they begin with the PNG signature (137, 80, 78, 71).

docs.title: Tracing
docs.text:
Tracing records screenshots and DOM snapshots for the lifetime of a context, then writes them to a zip that the Playwright trace viewer can open. This is what the BDD::Behave glue uses to dump a trace when an example fails.

Start tracing on the context before driving it, then stop and write the zip.

my $context = $browser.new-context;
$context.start-tracing;

my $page = $context.new-page;
$page.goto('file:///path/to/hello.html');
$page.locator('#go').click;

$context.stop-tracing(path => '/tmp/trace.zip');

Open the result with the Playwright CLI:

npx playwright show-trace /tmp/trace.zip

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: This walks through launching a browser, opening a page, navigating to a local file, and tearing everything down.

docs.title: Start the driver
docs.text:
WWW::Playwright.start spawns the Node sidecar and returns a driver handle.

use WWW::Playwright;

my $playwright = WWW::Playwright.start;

docs.title: Launch a browser
docs.text:
launch starts Chromium. It is headless by default; pass :!headless to see a window.

my $browser = $playwright.launch;          # headless
# my $browser = $playwright.launch(:!headless);

docs.title: Open a context and a page
docs.text:
A context is an isolated browser session, the right boundary for one test. Each context owns its own pages.

my $context = $browser.new-context;
my $page    = $context.new-page;

docs.title: Navigate
docs.text:
goto returns the HTTP status of the navigation response.

my $status = $page.goto('file:///path/to/hello.html');
say $status;   # 200

docs.title: Tear down
docs.text:
Close from the inside out, then stop the sidecar.

$page.close;
$context.close;
$browser.close;

$playwright.stop;

stop closes the sidecar's stdin, which closes any browser the sidecar still holds before the Node process exits.

docs.title: Installing
docs.text: WWW::Playwright drives a Node process, so it needs three things in place: Node itself, the playwright npm package, and the Chromium browser binary.

docs.title: Node
docs.text:
Install Node 18 or newer. The transport finds node on PATH; override the path with PLAYWRIGHT_NODE if needed (see Configuration).

node --version

docs.title: The Raku distribution
docs.text: zef install WWW::Playwright

docs.title: The npm package and the browser binary
docs.text:
After the Raku distribution is installed, run bin/install. It installs the pinned playwright npm package and the Chromium binary next to the sidecar script.

install        # the bin/install script shipped with the distribution

bin/install runs two steps in the sidecar home directory:

  1. npm install to fetch the pinned playwright package.
  2. npx playwright install chromium to download the browser binary.

docs.title: Where the sidecar home is
docs.text:
The sidecar home is the directory the sidecar resolves playwright from at runtime, and it depends on how the distribution is laid out:

  • Repo checkout (running with -Ilib): the home is resources/sidecar, where package.json and sidecar.mjs already live side by side. bin/install installs node_modules there.
  • Installed from zef: resources are stored content-addressed, so the sidecar script and package.json are not siblings. bin/install materializes them into a per-version cache directory (under $XDG_CACHE_HOME or ~/.cache) and installs node_modules there. The sidecar then runs from that cache copy, so Node resolves playwright next to it.

Either way, the directory bin/install writes to is the same one the transport checks at start, so a successful bin/install clears the DependenciesMissing error. Run bin/install once after each zef install or upgrade.

docs.title: What happens when a step is missing
docs.text:
• If the npm package is not installed, starting the sidecar throws X::WWW::Playwright::DependenciesMissing, pointing back at bin/install.
  • If the browser binary is not installed, launch throws X::WWW::Playwright::BrowserNotInstalled, also pointing at bin/install.

docs.title: Query reference
docs.text: Queries read state rather than mutating the page. Most run against a Locator; url and title run against the Page.

docs.title: url (Page)
docs.text:
The current page URL.

$page.url;        # 'file:///path/to/hello.html'

docs.title: title (Page)
docs.text:
The document title.

$page.title;      # 'Hello'

docs.title: text-content
docs.text:
The element's textContent.

$page.locator('#greeting').text-content;   # 'Hello, world'

docs.title: inner-text
docs.text:
The rendered innerText, reflecting visibility and CSS.

$page.locator('#greeting').inner-text;      # 'Hello, world'

docs.title: get-attribute
docs.text:
The value of a named attribute, or the type object when absent.

$page.locator('#name').get-attribute('type');   # 'text'

docs.title: input-value
docs.text:
The current value of an input, textarea, or select.

$page.locator('#name').input-value;

docs.title: count
docs.text:
The number of elements the locator matches.

$page.locator('#color option').count;       # 3

docs.title: is-visible, is-enabled, is-checked
docs.text:
Boolean state checks.

$page.locator('#greeting').is-visible;       # True
$page.locator('#name').is-enabled;           # True
$page.locator('#agree').is-checked;          # False until checked

docs.title: wait-for
docs.text:
Waits until the element reaches a state: attached, detached, visible, or hidden.

$page.locator('#greeting').wait-for(state => 'visible');

docs.title: Chaining
docs.text:
locator chains from a page or another locator, scoping the selector to the parent.

$page.locator('body').locator('#greeting').text-content;

docs.title: Node not found
docs.text:
Starting the sidecar throws X::WWW::Playwright::NodeNotFound:

Node binary 'node' not found. Install Node, or set PLAYWRIGHT_NODE to its path.

The transport could not find node on PATH. Install Node 18 or newer, or point PLAYWRIGHT_NODE at the binary:

PLAYWRIGHT_NODE=/opt/node/bin/node raku your-script.raku

docs.title: npm dependencies missing
docs.text:
Starting the sidecar throws X::WWW::Playwright::DependenciesMissing:

Sidecar npm dependencies are missing under <dir>. Run bin/install to install them.

The playwright npm package has not been installed next to the sidecar. Run bin/install, which runs npm install in the sidecar resources directory.

docs.title: Browser not installed
docs.text:
launch throws X::WWW::Playwright::BrowserNotInstalled:

The Chromium browser binary is not installed. Run bin/install to install it.

The npm package is present but the Chromium binary is not. Run bin/install, which also runs npx playwright install chromium.

docs.title: Sidecar crash
docs.text:
If the Node process dies mid-run, calls awaiting a response stay unresolved. Set PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG to stream the sidecar's stderr and see what it reported before exiting:

PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG=1 raku your-script.raku

The captured lines are also available on the transport through $sidecar.stderr-lines. See Configuration.

docs.title: Wire contract
docs.text: Raku and the Node sidecar speak JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio with NDJSON framing. Each message is a single line of compact JSON terminated by \n. The wire is never pretty-printed; a raw newline inside a message would break framing.

docs.title: Messages
docs.text:
Request:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"ping","params":{}}

Success response:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":"pong"}

Failure response:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32000,"message":"...","data":{"name":"Error","stack":"..."}}}

Every request carries a unique id. The sidecar echoes that id on the matching response so Raku can correlate replies to in-flight calls. Responses may arrive in any order; correlation is by id, not arrival order.

docs.title: Error codes
docs.text:
The sidecar follows the JSON-RPC 2.0 reserved range and adds one implementation-defined code for Playwright failures.

Code Meaning -32700 Parse error: the line was not valid JSON. -32600 Invalid request: missing or non-string method. -32601 Method not found. -32000 A Playwright or handler error was thrown.

A failure response carries the original error name and stack in data so the Raku side can rebuild a typed exception.

docs.title: Handle model
docs.text:
Browser objects never cross the wire. The sidecar keeps a registry mapping opaque string handles to live objects (a Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Locator, or ElementHandle). Handles are minted as kind@N:

browser@1
context@1
page@1
loc@7

A method that creates an object returns its handle. A later request passes the handle back in params.handle (or another named field) and the sidecar resolves it to the live object before acting. Resolving an unknown handle is an error.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
Method Params Result ping none "pong" launch { headless: Bool } a browser@N handle close { handle: "kind@N" } null

launch starts Chromium. close closes the object behind a handle and forgets it. When the sidecar's stdin closes, it closes every browser it launched before exiting.

docs.title: Browser
docs.text: A WWW::Playwright::Browser wraps a launched Chromium instance. Get one from WWW::Playwright.launch.

docs.title: new-context(--> Context)
docs.text:
Creates an isolated browser context and returns a Context. Each context has its own cookies, storage, and pages, which makes it the right boundary for one test.

my $context = $browser.new-context;

docs.title: close(--> Nil)
docs.text:
Closes the browser and every context and page under it.

$browser.close;

docs.title: Context
docs.text: A WWW::Playwright::Context is an isolated browser session. Get one from Browser.new-context.

docs.title: new-page(--> Page)
docs.text:
Opens a new page in the context and returns a Page.

my $page = $context.new-page;

docs.title: start-tracing(--> Nil)
docs.text:
Starts recording a trace (screenshots and DOM snapshots) for the context.

$context.start-tracing;

docs.title: stop-tracing(Str :$path --> Nil)
docs.text:
Stops tracing. With :path, writes the trace zip to that path.

$context.stop-tracing(path => '/tmp/trace.zip');

See Diagnostics for the full tracing workflow.

docs.title: close(--> Nil)
docs.text:
Closes the context and its pages.

$context.close;

docs.title: Locator
docs.text: A WWW::Playwright::Locator points at one or more elements matched by a selector. Get one from Page.locator or by chaining from another locator. Playwright auto-waits for the element before each action.

docs.title: locator(Str $selector --> Locator)
docs.text:
Returns a locator scoped to the matches of this one.

$page.locator('body').locator('#greeting');

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
Method Effect click(--> Nil) Clicks the element. fill(Str $value --> Nil) Replaces an input's value. type(Str $text --> Nil) Types text one key at a time. press(Str $key --> Nil) Dispatches a single key. check(--> Nil) Ticks a checkbox. uncheck(--> Nil) Clears a checkbox. select-option(Str $value --> List) Selects an option, returns chosen values. hover(--> Nil) Moves the pointer over the element.

See the Action reference for examples.

docs.title: Queries
docs.text:
Method Returns text-content(--> Str) The element's textContent. inner-text(--> Str) The rendered innerText. get-attribute(Str $name) A named attribute's value. input-value(--> Str) The current input/select value. count(--> Int) The number of matched elements. is-visible(--> Bool) Whether the element is visible. is-enabled(--> Bool) Whether the element is enabled. is-checked(--> Bool) Whether a checkbox is ticked.

See the Query reference for examples.

docs.title: wait-for(Str :$state --> Nil)
docs.text:
Waits until the element reaches a state: attached, detached, visible, or hidden.

$page.locator('#greeting').wait-for(state => 'visible');

docs.title: Page
docs.text: A WWW::Playwright::Page is a single tab. Get one from Context.new-page.

docs.title: goto(Str $url --> Int)
docs.text:
Navigates to a URL and returns the HTTP status of the navigation response.

my $status = $page.goto('file:///path/to/hello.html');   # 200

docs.title: url(--> Str)
docs.text:
The current page URL.

my $url = $page.url;   # 'file:///path/to/hello.html'

docs.title: title(--> Str)
docs.text:
The document title.

my $title = $page.title;   # 'Hello'

docs.title: locator(Str $selector --> Locator)
docs.text:
Returns a Locator for the selector. This is the entry point to every action and query.

my $heading = $page.locator('#greeting');

docs.title: screenshot(Str :$path --> Buf)
docs.text:
Captures the page as a PNG and returns the bytes. With :path, also writes the image to that path.

my $bytes = $page.screenshot(path => '/tmp/page.png');

See Diagnostics.

docs.title: close(--> Nil)
docs.text:
Closes the page.

$page.close;
